_README: User-maintained VERIFIED exchange FREEZE contacts. This file is the OVERRIDE layer: entries here win over the unverified pattern-style starter contacts in reports/legal_requests.py (_EXCHANGE_COMPLIANCE_CONTACTS). Add an entry once you have CONFIRMED the exchange's law-enforcement freeze channel, then set verified=true and record a source. NEVER set verified=true without a real channel (compliance_email or le_portal_url) AND a source - the loader will downgrade it to unverified and warn, and tests will fail on the committed file. Keys are exchange display names; matching is case- and space-insensitive against the names the tracer emits (e.g. "Binance", "Crypto.com", "OKX").
_provenance: v0.39 (2026-06): seeded from each exchange's OWN published law-enforcement guidance page (cited per-entry in `source`). verified=true is set ONLY where the channel was confirmed on the exchange's official LE page; entries confirmed only via legal/agreement docs or where the exact submission method could not be extracted are left verified=false with the researched channel populated as a confirm-before-send starting point. Kodex (app.kodexglobal.com) is the dominant LE-portal vendor (Binance/Coinbase/OKX/Crypto.com/Bitget/Bitfinex) and typically requires an agency (.gov/.police) email to register. RE-CONFIRM channels periodically: exchange LE pages and portal URLs change. Channels are an LE workflow aid, NOT legal advice.

<Exchange Display Name>.legal_name: string - registered legal entity to address the request to
<Exchange Display Name>.le_portal_url: string|null - verified law-enforcement request portal (preferred for most major CEXes)
<Exchange Display Name>.verified: boolean - true ONLY when you have confirmed the channel
<Exchange Display Name>.source: string|null - where/when you verified it (e.g. 'Binance LE Request Guide, 2026-06')
<Exchange Display Name>.notes: string|null - e.g. 'portal requires a .gov/agency email domain'

_example.source: confirmed via the exchange's published LE guide YYYY-MM-DD
_example.notes: Portal requires an agency email domain; freezes typically hold 30 days pending legal process.

Binance.legal_name: Binance Holdings Ltd. (group; regional entities vary)
Binance.notes: Kodex-hosted LERS portal; mark a case 'Exigent' for emergency/imminent-harm. China LE uses a separate link (app.kodexglobal.com/binance-cn/signup). Binance.US is a SEPARATE entity with its own LE guide at support.binance.us. No public LE email - submission is portal-only.

Coinbase.source: Coinbase legal/LE contact help page, https://help.coinbase.com (legal-policies: subpoena/legal-document contact), accessed 2026-06
Coinbase.notes: Criminal subpoenas / legal process via the Kodex gov portal; other LE questions to subpoenas@coinbase.com. Coinbase documents preservation/legal-process handling in its Transparency Report.

Kraken.source: Kraken 'How do I submit a legal inquiry' support article, https://support.kraken.com/articles/how-do-i-submit-a-legal-inquiry (accessed 2026-06)
Kraken.notes: Kraken routes Compliance/Legal/Privacy inquiries through a web form (Compliance & Legal support form, ID 648008). privacy@kraken.tech is for data-subject/privacy requests, NOT the LE channel. No standalone public freeze-on-request policy located.

OKX.legal_name: Aux Cayes FinTech Co. Ltd. (OKX group)
OKX.notes: Account-based portal (linked from the guide) plus enforcement@okx.com for all LE requests. Use 'URGENT REQUEST' in the subject for death/serious-danger exigent cases. A separate US guide exists (us-law-enforcement-request-guide).

Crypto.com.legal_name: Foris DAX, Inc. / Foris DAX MT (Malta) Ltd. (group entities vary)
Crypto.com.source: Crypto.com 'How can law enforcement agencies get in touch' help article #1360625, https://help.crypto.com (accessed 2026-06)
Crypto.com.notes: Crypto.com's help page states the Kodex portal is the dedicated global LE channel; acknowledges receipt with a reference number, ~30-day response target. The specific Kodex signup URL is not published on the help page - it links through.

KuCoin.legal_name: Mek Global Limited / Phoenixfin Pte. Ltd. (group)
KuCoin.notes: Primary channel = KuCoin Information Request System (kucoin.com/legal/requests); other LE to rbenquiries@kucoin.com; KuCoin EU uses lawenforcement@kucoin.eu. Requires a SIGNED/SEALED subpoena/court order/warrant (digital sig/seal NOT accepted). ~10-15 working-day internal investigation.

Gate.io.legal_name: Gate Technology Inc. / Gate Group (entities vary)
Gate.io.notes: All LE requests to regulatory@gate.io, in English, from an official .gov-domain email with proof of authority. ~5-15 working-day processing. Email-based (no separate portal stated).

Bitget.notes: Government LE Request Portal via Kodex (Chrome/Edge only); LE docs to lawenforcement@bitget.com, regulatory inquiries to regulatory@bitget.com. Signup requires an official agency email (.gov/.police; no Gmail/QQ). Generates a case number (BITGET-####); 'Emergency' legal-process type for urgent cases.

Bitfinex.legal_name: iFinex Inc. / BFXNA Inc. / BFXWW Inc.
Bitfinex.notes: Bitfinex has adopted Kodex to intake/verify LE requests (per Bitfinex's policy page + Kodex case study). compliance@bitfinex.com is the published compliance contact. Confirm whether the policy page links the current Kodex intake URL vs. email at time of use.

MEXC.legal_name: MEXC Global (operating entities vary; e.g. MEXC Global Ltd.)
MEXC.notes: Primary = MEXC Law Enforcement Online Request System (LEORS) at mexc.com/support/requests/legal (access approval ~3 business days); or legal@mexc.com. Documents data PRESERVATION for 90 days on formal legal request, extendable via LEORS - directly relevant to freeze/preservation. Warrant/court order required to compel disclosure.

Robinhood.legal_name: Robinhood Markets, Inc. (incl. Robinhood Crypto, LLC; Robinhood Securities, LLC)
Robinhood.source: Robinhood 'Information for Law Enforcement Authorities' PDF, https://cdn.robinhood.com/assets/robinhood/legal/Law-Enforcement-Request.pdf (accessed 2026-06)
Robinhood.notes: LE requests - including turnover of funds via attachment/garnishment/seizure - to LERequests@robinhood.com (explicitly NOT to support). ~45-day minimum response. Must identify the correct Robinhood entity (Crypto vs. Securities).

Robinhood Crypto.source: Robinhood 'Information for Law Enforcement Authorities' PDF, https://cdn.robinhood.com/assets/robinhood/legal/Law-Enforcement-Request.pdf (accessed 2026-06)
Robinhood Crypto.notes: Alias of the Robinhood entry so 'Robinhood Crypto' labels resolve. Same LERequests@robinhood.com channel.

Gemini.source: Gemini User/Custody Agreement legal docs, https://www.gemini.com/legal (accessed 2026-06)
Gemini.notes: CONFIRM BEFORE SENDING: lawenforcement@gemini.com surfaced from Gemini's legal/agreement docs (which document freezing/locking funds and complying with subpoenas/warrants/restraining orders), NOT from a dedicated LE-request guide page - left verified=false pending that confirmation. Mailing: Gemini Trust Company, LLC, 600 Third Avenue, 2nd Floor, New York, NY 10016.

Bitstamp.legal_name: Bitstamp Ltd. / Bitstamp Europe S.A. (Bitstamp by Robinhood)
Bitstamp.source: Bitstamp 'Guidelines for Law Enforcement Agencies' page, https://www.bitstamp.net/legal/guidelines-for-law-enforcement-agencies/ (page existence confirmed 2026-06)
Bitstamp.notes: CONFIRM BEFORE SENDING: the official LE guidelines page exists but the exact submission channel (email vs. portal) was not extractable - open the page to confirm before relying on it. Supports preservation requests. Now part of Robinhood.

Bybit.source: Bybit Help Center 'How to Report Stolen Assets', https://www.bybit.com/en/help-center/article/How-to-Report-Stolen-Assets (accessed 2026-06)
Bybit.notes: CONFIRM BEFORE SENDING: no dedicated official LE-request channel confirmed. Use the official help-center reporting flow; Bybit (Sept 2025) launched a VIP stolen-fund-inflow reporting portal that can place temporary internal restrictions. SECURITY WARNING: 'BYBIT159@amail.com' originates only from the non-official clone domain bybitexcai.com - it is a SCAM, do NOT use it. Verify any LE email directly with Bybit.
